description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, angular profile and a predominantly dark gray surface intersected by striking white mineral veins. The stone's surface is rough and uneven, with jagged edges and a natural, unpolished appearance. The white veining creates a dramatic contrast against the darker matrix, suggesting a metamorphic origin such as quartz-veined schist or gneiss. Some areas of the stone show a tan or beige coloration, likely from weathering or mineral inclusions. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its substantial size and weight make it suitable as a natural barrier or decorative feature in urban landscapes.
description: A large, rough, dark gray stone with bold white mineral veins and jagged edges, featuring some tan weathered areas. Its rugged, natural appearance makes it a striking urban obstacle or landscape element.
description_view_0: The broadest face displays prominent white veining across a dark gray surface, with jagged edges and a weathered tan section at the top.
description_view_1: A narrow side view reveals the stone's thickness and a small patch of white veining, with a visible label or marker on the upper surface.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face shows a similar pattern of white veins on dark gray, with more tan weathering along the upper edge.
description_view_3: The other narrow end highlights the stone's rough, tan-beige surface and blocky, uneven profile.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular, bold white veining crisscrosses the dark gray stone, creating a natural, high-contrast pattern typical of metamorphic rocks.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished with some tan discoloration, indicating moderate weathering and exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces or manicured lawns, but can blend with other natural stones or vegetation in landscaped areas.
